quote.text: In industries where one missed check can cost more than the entire project — accountability can't be an afterthought.

problem.label: The Problem
problem.heading: Quality failures cost
problem.subtext: The cost isn't just financial — it's structural, legal, and reputational.

items.title: Paper Checklists
items.description: No timestamps. No ownership. No traceability. Evidence disappears the moment it's filed.

items.title: Construction Failures
items.description: Unverified materials or alignment create legal exposure — and in the worst cases, structural failure.

items.title: Manufacturing Defects
items.description: One unchecked batch. No trail. Zero accountability. Recalls start exactly like this.

items.title: Reactive Quality
items.description: Catching failures after they happen costs 5x more. Prevention has to happen before the job is done.

creator.title: Creator
creator.description: builds the checklist, configures approval chains, assigns roles, and activates.
points: Configure L1 → L2 → L3 approval chain

filler.title: Fill & Submit
filler.description: opens assigned checklists, fills each item with evidence, and submits for approval.
points: Fill checklist items with photo, GPS & notes
Track status: Draft, In Progress, Approved, Rejected
Submit responses on web or mobile app

approver.title: Approve & Reject
approver.description: reviews submissions, signs off or rejects with reason, and raises snags for issues.
points: Approve with signature or reject with notes
Raise snags for minor issues without full rejection
Multi-level sign-off with timestamped audit trail

features.label: Platform Capabilities
features.subtext: Template to audit trail — every step of the inspection lifecycle tracked, enforced, and ready to export.

pdf.title: PDF Export & Full Audit Trail
pdf.description: Export any checklist as a structured PDF — all responses, sign-offs, snag logs, and a timestamped audit trail in one document.

snag.title: Snag-to-Checklist Workflow
snag.description: Raise, assign, and resolve defects without leaving the checklist. Every snag stays linked to the inspection it came from.

rules.title: Rule-Based Checklists
rules.description: Set rules at creation time. Tasks auto-assign by trade, zone, or schedule — no manual delegation, no missed assignments.

template.title: Template Library
template.description: Build once, deploy everywhere. Reusable templates with custom fields and approval chains — ready to launch across any site in seconds.

demo.title: Book a Demo

form.name: Name
form.messagePlaceholder: Describe your team, site locations, and current inspection process...

creator.question: What does the Creator role control?
creator.answer: The Creator (QC Manager / Supervisor) builds checklists from scratch or templates, configures L1→L2→L3 approval chains, assigns Fillers and Approvers, sets trades and task tags, and activates the checklist — instantly notifying the assigned team.

creator.question: Can we use our own checklist templates?
creator.answer: Absolutely. Build checklists from scratch or import your existing ones. Configure custom fields, trades, tasks, and approval chains to match your exact workflow. Templates can be reused across projects and sites.

creator.question: How do I assign roles and approval levels?
creator.answer: During checklist creation, you select team members for the Filler and Approver roles. You configure up to 3 approval levels — each level must explicitly sign off before the checklist progresses to the next.

creator.question: Which industries use QualityModule?
creator.answer: Primarily construction and manufacturing, but any industry with field inspections — infrastructure, energy, oil & gas, pharma — benefits from structured quality workflows.

filler.question: What evidence is required for submissions?
filler.answer: Every submission mandates a photo, GPS location, and detailed notes. The system won't accept submissions without this evidence — zero exceptions. This ensures complete traceability for every checklist item.

filler.question: Does QualityModule work offline on mobile?
filler.answer: Yes. The mobile app is designed for harsh field conditions with limited connectivity. You can capture evidence and fill checklists offline — everything syncs automatically when connection is restored.

filler.question: Can multiple Fillers work on the same checklist?
filler.answer: Each checklist item is assigned to a single Filler for clear accountability. However, different items within the same checklist can be assigned to different team members based on their trade or task.

filler.question: What happens after I submit a checklist?
filler.answer: Once submitted, the checklist moves to the approval queue. The assigned L1 Approver is notified instantly. You can track the approval status in real-time from your Filler dashboard.

approver.question: How does multi-level approval prevent errors?
approver.answer: You configure up to 3 approval levels (L1, L2, L3). Each level must explicitly approve or reject before the checklist progresses. Every action is timestamped with the approver's identity — building a complete audit trail.

approver.question: How does snag tracking work?
approver.answer: Approvers can raise Snags — minor issues that don't warrant full rejection. Each snag is tracked with a description, supporting media, and resolution status. Snags let you flag problems without blocking the entire workflow.

approver.question: What's included in the PDF audit export?
approver.answer: Full checklist with all responses, the complete L1/L2/L3 approval chain with timestamps, rejection reasons, snag logs, and media references — a single document ready for clients and auditors.

approver.question: How is QualityModule licensed?
approver.answer: We offer licence-based enterprise pricing — not per-user SaaS. Contact our sales team for a custom quote based on your team size and deployment needs.

footer.description: Built for field teams where a missed inspection has real consequences.
footer.tagline: Built for industries where quality isn't optional.
